scope.target: Converge command-style AI prompts into a top-level-skill plus sub-agent-role-prompt model and neutralize canonical skill references
scope.dependency_radius: .ai canonical assets, repo-local runtime wrappers, human-facing AI collaboration guides, workflow artifacts
constraints: Retain explanatory and shared prompt materials that still provide reusable guidance
Avoid creating a second canonical truth during reference migration
Use shell-based edits for .codex* paths because apply_patch fails on that path prefix in the current runtime
non_goals: Do not force all retained prompt materials to become skills immediately
Do not remove shared prompt materials without explicit classification

inputs.architecture_target: Make top-level skills the primary executable AI asset model while modeling delegated worker-role prompts as canonical sub-agent-role-prompts
user_constraints: Command-form prompts should be removed from the primary model and reclassified into the correct asset taxonomy
Explanatory prompts that help AI collaboration should remain unless explicitly judged otherwise
This work should be tracked as a refactor workflow with checklist artifacts
Codex repo-local skills work without sync/export tooling
Sub-agent style use cases should have an explicit canonical definition source plus a dedicated delegated-wrapper strategy when needed

steps: Validate that .codex/commands removal leaves no active references
Inventory command-spec assets and prompt inventory
Classify prompts into migrate-to-skill, keep-as-shared, or user-judgment-needed
Plan canonical skill reference migration from .claude paths to .ai/assets/skills/.../references
Update strategy and index docs as migration decisions are executed
Partial phase 1 complete: copied three reference sets into .ai/assets/skills/.../references and updated three canonical skill specs
Boundary confirmed: apply_patch fails on .codex* paths in this runtime, so .codex wrapper edits must use shell-based replacement
Phase 1 completed: repointed three Codex skill wrappers to canonical reference paths and validated the remaining active references
Phase 2 started: created skill-first replacements for command/query/reactor and added matching Codex runtime wrappers
Marked the legacy command-spec layer as deprecated and linked command/query/reactor specs to their new skill replacements
Updated active architecture docs to describe use-case sub-agents as skill-first assets with explicit canonical-source and wrapper roles
Finalized the Claude/Codex wrapper strategy by treating .ai/assets/skills as the canonical source and adding Claude thin wrappers for command/query/reactor
Re-labeled remaining active legacy command-spec and prompt-package references so they explicitly point to skill-first primary entries
Design correction applied: command/query/reactor are now recognized as sub-agent role prompts, not top-level skills
Created canonical sub-agent-role-prompt assets and repointed active docs and compatibility wrappers to the corrected canonical source
Rolled back the mistaken command/query/reactor top-level skills and removed their Codex/Claude skill wrappers
Completed the remaining aggregate/frontend/mutation-testing/outbox/profile-config sub-agent conversions into canonical sub-agent-role-prompt assets
Updated active frontend prompts and human guides to point to canonical sub-agent-role-prompt assets instead of the old prompt files
Accepted the skill-versus-sub-agent boundary in ADR-050 and converted delegated review/test-generation prompts into canonical sub-agent-role-prompt assets
Converted controller-test-generation into a canonical sub-agent-role-prompt asset and classified contract-test-generation as retained supporting material
Converted controller-code-review and reactor-code-review into canonical delegated review sub-agent assets
Locked the retention strategy for shared and explanatory prompt materials and updated wrapper/index documentation to match the new taxonomy
validation: No active repository path depends on .codex/commands
Each sub-agent-style prompt family has a canonical asset or an explicit retirement decision
Each retained explanatory prompt has a justified long-term role
Canonical skill specs no longer point to .claude/skills/.../references after migration
deferred_items: Design new specialized top-level skills only after the remaining shared/supporting prompt inventory is classified
Decide the final fate of judgment-required prompt materials with user input where needed
Clean remaining historical docs, ADRs, and scripts that still mention the old command/query/reactor prompt-first model
Decide when the legacy command-spec and legacy prompt-package compatibility layer can be fully removed
Define whether delegated sub-agent wrappers need their own dedicated runtime directories beyond canonical sub-agent role prompt assets
Clean historical references that still mention the removed legacy command-spec and prompt-package layer
Revisit whether contract-test-generation-prompt should become a future DBC-focused sub-agent asset after Design by Contract standards mature

results.summary: Planning is committed, Stage G Phase 1 is complete, and the taxonomy correction now covers all current sub-agent-style prompt families: active docs point to canonical sub-agent-role-prompt assets, and the mistaken top-level skill wrappers have been rolled back.
